AIOps - HealthCheck Runner
__MAIN_MENU__

HealthCheck Design Logic

__DOC_VERSION__ | __DOC_RULE__
Back to Docs

Outline

1. Business Goals 2. Overall Architecture 3. Page Workflow 4. Execution Logic 5. AI Analysis Design 6. File Hierarchy 7. Deployment Dependencies 8. Key Changes (V2.4) 9. Key Design Points 10. Security and Ops Notes

1. Business Goals

This system is for network engineers, integrating multi-device inspection, structured reporting, and AI diagnosis into one workflow.

  • Batch inspection: execute mapped checks and custom commands across many devices.
  • Report retention: output JSON/CSV for audit and comparison.
  • Intelligent diagnosis: supports ChatGPT, DeepSeek, Gemini, NVIDIA, and local LLMs.

2. Overall Architecture

The architecture has four layers: Web UI, request orchestration, AI services, and inspection execution.

  • UI layer: form inputs, job status polling, and AI trigger.
  • Web orchestration: `web_server.py` handles routing, auth, parsing, and page render.
  • AI services: `analysis_service.py`, `status_service.py`, `llm_adapter.py`.
  • Execution layer: SSH login, command execution, retry/statistics, report write.
  • Docs service: `docs_service.py` + `docs/templates/zh|en/*`, decoupling docs from server code.

3. Page Workflow

Submit from home page -> jump to job status page -> download reports and run AI analysis.

  • Home: devices, checks, command_map, execution options.
  • Check templates: save current selected checks + custom commands, then review/edit/delete.
  • Status page: live logs, status badge, report download.
  • AI analysis: use historical report first; otherwise use current report.

4. Execution Logic

The core executor is `app/healthcheck.py`; Web launches it as subprocess with stdin parameters.

  • Device sanitization: deduplication, format checks, invalid filtering.
  • Command sources: mapped intents + custom commands in input order.
  • Concurrency strategy: auto/serial/parallel with workers.
  • Report outputs: `output/reports/inspection_report_*.json|csv`.

5. AI Analysis Design

A two-layer prompt strategy is used for strict output: System Prompt controls behavior, Task Prompt defines focus.

  • System prompt: evidence chain required, no guessing, fixed output structure.
  • Task prompt: focuses on interface/protocol/resource scenarios.
  • Extra inputs: extra system constraints + extra task requirements.
  • Precheck: estimates device count, calls, token usage, and time before execution.
  • Normal mode: key evidence + controlled truncation to avoid context overflow.
  • Batch mode: per-device analysis with global summary, supports device-level concurrency and retries.
  • Chunk mode: per-device chunked analysis by check items, then device/global summarization.
  • Unified model invocation: model discovery + candidate match + endpoint fallback.
  • Token metrics: current token and cumulative token.
  • Result export: save analysis report as Markdown (.md).

6. File Hierarchy

healthcheck/ app/ # core programs web_server.py # Web orchestration (main entry) analysis_service.py# AI orchestration service status_service.py # analysis status service llm_adapter.py # LLM adapter llm_service.py # provider API calls healthcheck.py # inspection executor web_runner.py # compatibility entry config/ # command_map.yaml data/ # devices.txt / intents.txt docs/ # readme.md docs/templates/ # help-doc templates (zh/en) output/reports/ # inspection JSON/CSV runtime/tmp/ # runtime temp files state/ # gpt_config.json / token_stats.json run.sh # recommended unified startup entry (venv/deps/port cleanup/hot reload) scripts/ # startup scripts (start_web.sh / dev_reload.py) prompts/ system_default/ system_custom/ task_default/ task_custom/

7. Deployment Dependencies

  • Python 3.9+ (recommended 3.10/3.11).
  • Python packages: `paramiko`, `PyYAML`, optional `certifi`.
  • System capability: SSH access to devices and network access to model APIs.
  • Port: default `8080`, configurable by `HC_WEB_PORT`.
  • Writable directories: `output/reports`, `runtime/tmp`, `state`, `prompts/*`.
  • Required config: `config/command_map.yaml` (or upload from UI).
  • Local LLM: ensure `base_url` reachable and model loaded.
  • For CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED, set CA bundle via startup script.
python3 -m venv .venv source .venv/bin/activate pip install -U pip pip install paramiko PyYAML certifi python -m pip install -r requirements.txt ./scripts/start_web.sh

8. Key Changes (V2.4)

  • Top navigation/header style is fully aligned with Netlog Extractor across pages.
  • AI settings button names are shortened to: `Import API Key`, `Test Connection`, `Save Config`.
  • Added unified launcher `run.sh` (auto venv/dependency setup, port cleanup, hot reload).
  • Help pages now share the same navigation style and language-switch behavior as main pages.

V2.3 changes are retained below:

  • "Save Analysis Report" supports markdown export (.md).
  • AI service split completed: `analysis_service.py`, `status_service.py`, `llm_adapter.py`.
  • Analysis precheck added for early risk detection (calls/token/time).
  • Help docs fully extracted from `web_server.py` to template-based structure.

9. Key Design Points

  • Relative-path first to improve portability.
  • Template maintainability: import/edit/delete with confirmation.
  • Check templates store selected checks + custom commands for quick reuse.
  • Observability: status polling with optional debug output.
  • Traceability: report and AI output retain source/model metadata.
  • Language linkage: help docs follow UI language.

10. Security and Ops Notes

  • API keys are stored locally in `state/gpt_config.json`; protect directory permissions.
  • Exclude `state/`, `output/reports/`, `runtime/tmp/` from git.
  • In production, pin strict system prompts to reduce output drift.
For enterprise use, consider adding change audit and incident knowledge base sections.
